name: Button
description: Primary action trigger with multiple tones and sizes.

disabled.description: Disables interaction and applies disabled styling.

a11yNotes: Set descriptive text content.
Avoid icon-only buttons unless aria-label is provided.

positive: Use for primary and secondary actions.
Prefer `variant=secondary` for non-destructive secondary actions.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Button } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Button variant='primary'>Save</Button>

name: Input
description: Text input field for forms and filters.
a11yNotes: Use with `<label>` or `aria-label`.
Describe validation states with helper or error text.

positive: Wrap in FormField when label/hint/error text is needed.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Input } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Input placeholder='Email address' />

name: IconButton
description: Compact icon-only button for toolbars and contextual actions.
a11yNotes: Always provide a meaningful `label` for screen readers.
Use danger tone only for destructive actions.

positive: Use in dense nav bars, command rows, and table action columns.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

name: Textarea
description: Multiline text input for long-form content and notes.

onChange.description: Callback fired for on change events.

a11yNotes: Pair with an explicit label or `aria-label`.
Show helper text for character limits when applicable.

positive: Use for feedback forms, release notes, and message composers.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Textarea } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Textarea rows={5} placeholder='Add details' />

name: Select
description: Native select control wrapped with Zephr tokens and spacing.

onChange.description: Callback fired for on change events.

children.description: Nested content rendered inside the component.

a11yNotes: Use a visible label and descriptive option text.
Avoid using select for very long lists without search.

positive: Use in filters, settings, and configuration forms.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Select } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Select><option>notion</option><option>stripe</option></Select>

name: Checkbox
description: Binary form control for multi-select preferences.

onChange.description: Callback fired for on change events.

a11yNotes: Wrap with label text for accessible interaction.
Group related checkboxes in a fieldset when possible.

positive: Use for settings toggles that can be independently selected.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Checkbox } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<label><Checkbox /> Enable notifications</label>

name: Radio
description: Single-choice selector inside mutually exclusive option groups.

onChange.description: Callback fired for on change events.

a11yNotes: Share the same `name` value within each radio group.
Use fieldset and legend for grouped context.

positive: Use for plan/theme choices where only one option is allowed.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Radio } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<label><Radio name='theme' /> notion</label>

name: Switch
description: Accessible toggle switch for immediate on/off states.

onChange.description: Callback fired for on change events.

a11yNotes: Renders `role=switch` with keyboard support for Enter and Space.
Use concise labels describing current behavior.

positive: Use for feature flags and preference toggles.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Switch } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Switch checked={enabled} onChange={setEnabled} label='Enable beta' />

name: Badge
description: Small status label for metadata, state, and tags.

children.description: Nested content rendered inside the component.

a11yNotes: Do not rely only on badge color to communicate meaning.
Include readable text in every badge.

positive: Use for item status, release labels, and category chips.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Badge } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Badge tone='success'>Synced</Badge>

name: Avatar
description: User identity image with deterministic text fallback.
a11yNotes: Pass a human-readable `name` for the image alt/aria label.
Keep avatar size large enough for recognition in dense UI.

positive: Use in nav headers, comments, tables, and collaboration views.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Avatar } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Avatar name='Akhil Krishnan' />

name: Logo
description: Brand logo image with initials fallback and tokenized shape.
a11yNotes: Set `name` to provide meaningful logo alt text.
Use compliance-approved sources when rendering third-party logos.

positive: Use in ecosystem cards, integration lists, and partner sections.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Logo } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Logo name='OpenAI' src={logoDataUri} />

name: FormField
description: Label, control, hint, and error wrapper for form inputs.
a11yNotes: Links label and control using `htmlFor`/`id` pairing.
Expose validation errors in readable text below the control.

positive: Use for every labeled form input block in onboarding and settings.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { FormField, Input } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<FormField label='Email' htmlFor='email'><Input id='email' /></FormField>

name: SearchBox
description: Input + submit action for query workflows.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired for on value change events.

onSearch.description: Callback fired for on search events.

a11yNotes: Includes form semantics for Enter-to-search keyboard flow.

positive: Use near table/list headers to drive filtering.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { SearchBox } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<SearchBox value={query} onValueChange={setQuery} onSearch={runSearch} />

name: Navbar
description: Top-level navigation with brand, links, and action slot.
a11yNotes: Use inside `<nav>` for semantic navigation landmarks.

positive: Use as top-level layout shell in admin and SaaS products.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples: import { Navbar } from '@zephrui/ui-react'; Zephr} links={[{ id: 'home', label: 'Home', href: '/' }]} />

name: Header
description: Section header with title, subtitle, and actions slot.
a11yNotes: Prefer one primary heading per section hierarchy.
Keep action labels explicit when multiple controls are present.

positive: Use above card/table sections to establish context and actions.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Header, Button } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Header title='Workspace' subtitle='Manage your project' actions={<Button>New</Button>} />

name: DataTable
description: Typed data table with custom rendering per column.
a11yNotes: Use `<th>` headers and keep row actions keyboard accessible.

positive: Pair with SearchBox and FiltersBar for interactive data screens.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { DataTable } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<DataTable data={rows} columns={columns} rowKey={(row) => row.id} />

name: ModalDialog
description: Confirmation and workflow modal with controlled open state.

onCancel.description: Callback fired for on cancel events.

onConfirm.description: Callback fired for on confirm events.

a11yNotes: Uses dialog semantics and backdrop click-to-close.
Ensure focus management in host application if nested flows are used.

positive: Use for destructive confirmations and short multi-step flows.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { ModalDialog } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<ModalDialog open={open} title='Delete item' onCancel={close} onConfirm={remove} />

name: Tabs
description: Segmented content switcher with accessible tab semantics.

initialTabId.description: Text value for initial tab id.

a11yNotes: Uses `tablist`, `tab`, and `tabpanel` semantics.

positive: Use for compact section switching in settings and dashboards.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Tabs } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Tabs items={[{ id: 'overview', label: 'Overview', content: <div /> }]} />

name: Accordion
description: Collapsible content sections for FAQs, settings, and guided documentation.

items.description: Accordion item list with id/title/content metadata.

a11yNotes: Uses button semantics with `aria-expanded` and `aria-controls`.
Each open panel is announced via a labeled region.

positive: Use for FAQ sections and progressive reveal flows.
Prefer concise titles and short supporting descriptions.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Accordion } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Accordion items={[{ id: 'q1', title: 'What is Zephr?', description: 'A plug-and-play UI framework.' }]} />

name: Alert
description: Inline feedback banner with status, action link, and dismiss control.
a11yNotes: Rendered with `role=status` for assistive technologies.
Do not rely only on color to communicate message meaning.

positive: Use for system feedback and policy notices.
Use stroke style in dense layouts to reduce visual weight.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Alert } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Alert status='error' variant='filled' title='Something went wrong' actionLabel='Retry' />

name: Toast
description: Transient status surface optimized for notifications and AI action feedback.

actionLabel.description: Optional CTA rendered on the right.

onClose.description: Callback fired for on close events.

a11yNotes: When multiple toasts are shown, keep order predictable and manageable.
Use concise text so announcements are quickly understood.

positive: Use for async completion, copy confirmations, and background sync notices.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Toast } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Toast open status='success' title='Prompt copied' onClose={() => {}} />

name: Breadcrumbs
description: Hierarchical navigation trail for deep pages.
a11yNotes: Rendered inside a nav landmark with `aria-label='Breadcrumb'.

positive: Use on docs and admin pages above the main title.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Breadcrumbs } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Breadcrumbs items={[{ id: 'home', label: 'Home', href: '/' }]} />

name: Pagination
description: Previous/next navigation with page count context.

onPageChange.description: Callback fired for on page change events.

a11yNotes: Buttons expose disabled state for first/last boundaries.

positive: Use below tables and result lists.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Pagination } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Pagination page={page} totalPages={10} onPageChange={setPage} />

name: CommandBar
description: Inline command launcher with query input and action buttons.

onQueryChange.description: Callback fired for on query change events.

a11yNotes: Uses form semantics for Enter-to-run on primary action.

positive: Use in command palettes and quick actions headers.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { CommandBar } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<CommandBar query={query} onQueryChange={setQuery} actions={actions} />

name: InputGroup
description: Input field with optional leading/trailing adornments.
a11yNotes: Pair with labels or aria-label for form accessibility.

positive: Use for currency, search, and prefixed text fields.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { InputGroup } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<InputGroup startAdornment='$' placeholder='Budget' />

name: Dropdown
description: Compact disclosure menu using native details/summary.
a11yNotes: Menu entries are focusable buttons for keyboard support.

positive: Use for overflow actions and contextual menus.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { Dropdown } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Dropdown label='Actions' items={[{ id: 'edit', label: 'Edit', onSelect: () => {} }]} />

name: SidebarNav
description: Sectioned vertical navigation for docs/admin layouts.

sections.type: Array<{ id, title, items: Array<{ id, label, href, active? }> }>

a11yNotes: Use within an aside landmark and ensure clear active indication.

positive: Use for persistent left navigation in documentation and apps.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { SidebarNav } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<SidebarNav sections={sections} />

name: FiltersBar
description: Horizontal filter controls row with optional reset action.

onReset.description: Callback fired for on reset events.

a11yNotes: Filter controls should expose labels and keyboard interaction.

positive: Pair with DataTable and SearchBox on data screens.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { FiltersBar } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<FiltersBar items={filters} onReset={resetFilters} />

name: SearchResultsPanel
description: Query summary with selectable search result list.

results.type: Array<{ id, title, description?, metadata?, onSelect? }>

a11yNotes: Each result row is rendered as an interactive button.

positive: Use in command/search experiences and discovery pages.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { SearchResultsPanel } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<SearchResultsPanel query={query} results={results} />

name: LayoutShell
description: Page scaffold with top nav, sidebar, content area, and optional right rail.

children.description: Nested content rendered inside the component.

a11yNotes: Use semantic landmarks (`nav`, `aside`, `main`) for better screen reader navigation.

positive: Use as a starting point for docs portals and admin dashboards.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { LayoutShell, Navbar } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<LayoutShell topNav={<Navbar brand='Zephr' links={[]} />}>...</LayoutShell>

name: IconLibrary
description: Searchable Material icon browser with style variants and copy-on-click behavior.

initialQuery.description: Initial search query for icon discovery.

onCopy.description: Callback fired for on copy events.

a11yNotes: Keep icon cards keyboard focusable and expose icon names in readable text.
Use explicit labels for icon-only UI controls after insertion.

positive: Use for icon pickers, command palettes, and design handoff tooling.
Support semantic search terms (e.g., settings, billing, support).
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { IconLibrary } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<IconLibrary initialQuery='settings' onCopy={(name) => console.log(name)} />

name: AvatarLibrary
description: Searchable in-house avatar style browser with deterministic style previews.

sampleName.description: Display name used to render avatar previews.

onCopy.description: Callback fired for on copy events.

a11yNotes: Display style names as text labels so previews are understandable without color.
Ensure copied style ids are announced with toast or status text.

positive: Use for profile setup flows and collaborative presence pickers.
Expose style + seed controls for deterministic generation.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { AvatarLibrary } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<AvatarLibrary initialQuery='retro' onCopy={(style) => console.log(style)} />

name: LogoLibrary
description: Searchable first-party brand logo browser with domain copy workflow.

onCopy.description: Callback fired for on copy events.

a11yNotes: Show brand name and domain text next to each logo tile.
Do not rely on color alone to distinguish brands.

positive: Use in integration galleries, partner lists, and asset pickers.
Allow searching by brand name, domain, and category tags.
negative: Avoid raw HTML controls when a Zephr component exists.

usageExamples:
import { LogoLibrary } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<LogoLibrary initialQuery='developer' onCopy={(domain) => console.log(domain)} />

name: Card
description: Surface container with optional elevation, border, and padding presets.

asChild.description: Render as a child element instead of a div.

a11yNotes: If the card is interactive, use a button or anchor as the root element via asChild.

positive: Use Card to group related content in dashboards, list items, and detail panels.
Combine with Stack for vertical content layout inside the card.
negative: Avoid nesting Cards more than one level deep.

usageExamples:
import { Card } from '@zephrui/ui-react';

Content

name: Divider
description: Horizontal or vertical separator line for visual sectioning.
a11yNotes: Use role='separator' for decorative dividers; hide from screen readers with aria-hidden='true' when purely visual.

positive: Use to separate form sections, list groups, or navigation items.
negative: Avoid using as a layout spacer — use Spacer instead.

name: Progress
description: Linear progress bar showing completion percentage or indeterminate state.
a11yNotes: Always include a descriptive label or aria-label. Set role='progressbar' with aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax.

positive: Use in file upload flows, onboarding steps, and task completion screens.
negative: Avoid animating to 100% without a subsequent success state.

usageExamples:
import { Progress } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Progress value={72} label='Uploading…' />

name: Skeleton
description: Loading placeholder that mimics content shape while data is fetching.
a11yNotes: Wrap skeleton groups in a container with aria-busy='true' and aria-label='Loading'.

positive: Match skeleton dimensions to the real content to prevent layout shift.
Compose multiple skeletons to mirror complex card or list layouts.
negative: Don't show skeletons for instant operations under 200ms.

name: Slider
description: Range input for selecting a numeric value within a min–max interval.
a11yNotes: Always provide a label. The slider renders a native range input with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow.

positive: Use for volume controls, price range filters, opacity settings, and numeric configuration.
negative: Avoid for precise numeric entry — use NumberInput instead.

usageExamples:
import { Slider } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Slider label='Volume' min={0} max={100} defaultValue={50} onChange={(v) => setVolume(v)} />

name: Tooltip
description: Short contextual hint that appears on hover or focus near a trigger element.
a11yNotes: Tooltip content must not be the sole means of conveying critical information. Trigger must be focusable.

positive: Wrap icon-only buttons with Tooltip to expose the action label.
Use side='bottom' for toolbar items to avoid clipping the viewport top.
negative: Don't put interactive content (links, buttons) inside a Tooltip — use Popover instead.

usageExamples:
import { Tooltip } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Tooltip content='Delete item'><IconButton icon='delete' aria-label='Delete' /></Tooltip>

name: Popover
description: Floating panel anchored to a trigger, used for menus, pickers, and rich contextual UI.
a11yNotes: Popover traps focus when open. Press Escape to close. The trigger should have aria-expanded and aria-controls.

positive: Use for color pickers, date pickers, filter panels, and rich dropdowns.
Compose Popover with a form for inline editing flows.
negative: Avoid deeply nested popovers — prefer a modal for complex interactions.

usageExamples:
import { Popover } from '@zephrui/ui-react'; Open}>
Popover content

name: ComboBox
description: Searchable select with async option loading, custom rendering, and free-text support.
a11yNotes: Uses combobox ARIA pattern with listbox. Keyboard: type to filter, arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to close.

positive: Use ComboBox when options exceed 10 items or require async search.
Set onInputChange to debounce API calls for remote search.
negative: Prefer Select for short static option lists under 10 items.

usageExamples:
import { ComboBox } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<ComboBox options={users} value={selected} onChange={setSelected} placeholder='Search users…' />

name: ButtonGroup
description: Visually connected set of buttons sharing a single border, used for segmented controls.
a11yNotes: Wrap in a div with role='group' and aria-label describing the group purpose.

positive: Use for view switchers (List / Grid), text alignment controls, and time range selectors.
Mark the active button with aria-pressed='true'.
negative: Avoid more than 5 buttons in a group — use Tabs instead.

usageExamples:
import { ButtonGroup } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<ButtonGroup>
  <Button>Day</Button>
  <Button>Week</Button>
  <Button>Month</Button>
</ButtonGroup>

name: DatePicker
description: Calendar-based date or date-range selection with input trigger.
a11yNotes: Calendar grid uses aria-grid. Each day cell has aria-label with full date. Keyboard: arrow keys to navigate days, Enter to select.

positive: Wrap in FormField for consistent label and error display.
Use minDate/maxDate to restrict selection to valid business windows.
negative: Don't use for year-only or time-only selection — use a dedicated input.

usageExamples:
import { DatePicker } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<DatePicker label='Due date' value={date} onChange={setDate} />

name: ColorPicker
description: Inline or popover color picker with hex input, hue slider, and preset swatches.
a11yNotes: Provide an aria-label on the swatch trigger. Hex input is keyboard accessible. Ensure sufficient contrast on the selected color preview.

positive: Use for theme pickers, avatar background selection, and annotation tools.
Provide presets matching the active style pack accent palette.
negative: Avoid inline color pickers in dense forms — use a popover trigger instead.

usageExamples:
import { ColorPicker } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<ColorPicker label='Accent' value={color} onChange={setColor} presets={['#2563eb', '#16a34a', '#dc2626']} />

name: NumberInput
description: Numeric input with increment/decrement stepper buttons, min/max clamping, and format support.
a11yNotes: Stepper buttons have aria-label 'Increment' and 'Decrement'. Wrap in FormField for a visible label.

positive: Use for quantity selectors, pagination page sizes, and port/timeout config fields.
Use formatValue to display units (e.g. '50px', '$12.00').
negative: Avoid for unconstrained large numbers — use a plain Input with type='number'.

usageExamples:
import { NumberInput } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<NumberInput label='Quantity' min={1} max={99} defaultValue={1} onChange={setQty} />

name: TagInput
description: Multi-value input that converts typed text into removable tag chips on Enter.
a11yNotes: Each tag chip has an accessible remove button with aria-label. The input has role='textbox' with aria-label matching the field label.

positive: Use for email CC fields, category labels, and keyword/skills tagging.
Use validate to prevent duplicates or enforce format constraints.
negative: Avoid for single-value selection — use Select or ComboBox instead.

usageExamples:
import { TagInput } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<TagInput label='Tags' defaultValue={['design', 'react']} onChange={setTags} />

name: Sheet
description: Slide-over panel that appears from the edge of the screen, typically for settings and detail views.
a11yNotes: Sheet traps focus when open. Has role='dialog' with aria-label. Escape key closes it. Backdrop click also closes by default.

positive: Use for detail editing, filter panels, and step-through flows where context from the main view should remain visible.
Prefer right-side sheets for detail views and left-side for navigation.
negative: Avoid using Sheet for confirmation dialogs — use AlertDialog instead.

usageExamples:
import { Sheet } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Sheet open={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} title='Edit profile' side='right'><form>…</form></Sheet>

name: AlertDialog
description: Blocking confirmation dialog for destructive or irreversible actions requiring explicit user consent.
a11yNotes: Has role='alertdialog'. Focus moves to the Cancel button on open. Escape key triggers onCancel. Backdrop click is blocked to prevent accidental dismissal.

positive: Use for delete, archive, and revoke operations.
Set tone='danger' for irreversible destructive actions.
negative: Don't use AlertDialog for informational messages — use Alert or Toast instead.
Don't add complex forms inside AlertDialog — use a Sheet or ModalDialog.

usageExamples:
import { AlertDialog } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<AlertDialog open={open} title='Delete project?' description='This cannot be undone.' tone='danger' onConfirm={handleDelete} onCancel={() => setOpen(false)} />

name: RichEditor
description: Full-featured rich text editor with toolbar, markdown shortcuts, and slash commands.
a11yNotes: Editor area has role='textbox' and aria-multiline='true'. Toolbar buttons have descriptive aria-labels. Keyboard shortcuts follow common editor conventions (Ctrl+B, Ctrl+I).

positive: Use for note-taking, blog post editors, email composers, and documentation fields.
Wrap in FormField to include a label and validation error.
negative: Avoid for single-line or plain-text inputs — use Input or Textarea instead.

usageExamples:
import { RichEditor } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<RichEditor placeholder='Write your post…' defaultValue={draft} onChange={setDraft} />

name: Stack
description: Flex layout primitive for stacking children vertically or horizontally with uniform gap.
a11yNotes: Stack renders a div by default. Use asChild to merge onto a semantic element like section or nav.

positive: Use Stack as the primary layout primitive inside Cards, form sections, and page columns.
Prefer Stack over ad-hoc flex CSS for consistent spacing with design tokens.
negative: Don't use Stack for complex 2D grid layouts — use Grid instead.

usageExamples:
import { Stack } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Stack direction='vertical' gap='4'><Header /><Content /><Footer /></Stack>

name: Grid
description: CSS grid layout primitive with responsive column configuration and token-based gaps.

columns.description: Number of columns or CSS grid-template-columns value.

a11yNotes: Grid renders a div. Ensure grid children use semantic HTML where appropriate.

positive: Use Grid for card galleries, stat grids, feature lists, and multi-column forms.
Set columns='repeat(auto-fill, minmax(240px, 1fr))' for responsive grids.
negative: Don't use Grid for simple single-axis layouts — use Stack instead.

usageExamples:
import { Grid } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<Grid columns={3} gap='6'>{items.map(item => <Card key={item.id}>{item.name}</Card>)}</Grid>

name: Box
description: Base layout block for applying spacing, background, radius, and border via design tokens.
a11yNotes: Box renders a div. Use asChild to merge onto semantic elements.

positive: Use Box for quick one-off spacing, backgrounds, or border wrappers without creating a new component.
Combine with Stack for padded flex sections.
negative: Don't overuse Box for complex layouts — prefer dedicated layout components.

usageExamples:
import { Box } from '@zephrui/ui-react';

Content

name: Spacer
description: Flexible spacer that expands to fill available space or adds a fixed gap between elements.

size.description: Fixed size token. Omit for flex: 1 expansion.

a11yNotes: Spacer renders an aria-hidden div and has no semantic meaning.

positive: Use inside Stack to push trailing actions to the end of a row (e.g. title on left, button on right).
Use a fixed size Spacer between sections as an alternative to margin-top.
negative: Don't use Spacer for fine-grained padding — adjust the parent Stack gap instead.

usageExamples:
import { Stack, Spacer, Button } from '@zephrui/ui-react';

Title

name: DashboardPage
description: Full-page analytics dashboard with metric cards, data table, and chart placeholders.
a11yNotes: All metric cards have aria-label with label and value. Chart placeholders include a data table fallback for screen readers.

positive: Use as the root page for SaaS product home screens, analytics portals, and admin panels.
Customize metric labels and chart sections for the specific product domain.
negative: Don't use as a sub-page layout — use LayoutShell for nested views.

usageExamples:
import { DashboardPage } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<DashboardPage title='Analytics' metrics={[{ label: 'MRR', value: '$42,800', delta: '+12%' }]} />

name: AuthPage
description: Authentication page with login, signup, and password reset flows in a centered card layout.
a11yNotes: Form has a unique aria-label per mode. Error messages are announced via aria-live. Social auth buttons have descriptive aria-labels.

positive: Use mode='signup' for registration, mode='login' for returning users.
Pass oauthProviders to show social login buttons above the form.
negative: Don't add custom fields directly — extend via the children slot inside the form card.

usageExamples:
import { AuthPage } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<AuthPage mode='login' oauthProviders={['google', 'github']} onSubmit={handleLogin} />

name: SettingsPage
description: Tabbed settings page with sections for profile, account, notifications, billing, and security.
a11yNotes: Tab panel uses ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel pattern. Keyboard: arrow keys to switch tabs, Tab to move into panel content.

positive: Use for user preferences, workspace configuration, and billing management pages.
Pass custom tabs to restrict which settings sections are visible per user role.
negative: Don't put critical account actions (delete, downgrade) in a top-level tab — nest them inside a Danger Zone section.

usageExamples:
import { SettingsPage } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<SettingsPage defaultTab='profile' tabs={[{ id: 'profile', label: 'Profile', content: <ProfileForm /> }]} />

name: OnboardingPage
description: Multi-step onboarding wizard with progress indicator, step validation, and completion state.
a11yNotes: Step progress is announced via aria-live. Each step panel has a unique aria-labelledby. Back/Next buttons are always visible and keyboard accessible.

positive: Use for new user setup, workspace configuration wizards, and feature activation flows.
Keep steps focused — 3–5 steps is ideal. Use onComplete to navigate to the dashboard.
negative: Avoid using OnboardingPage for re-occurring tasks — use a modal or sheet for repeated actions.

usageExamples:
import { OnboardingPage } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<OnboardingPage title='Set up your workspace' steps={steps} onComplete={() => router.push('/dashboard')} />

name: MarketingPage
description: Public-facing marketing landing page with hero, features, pricing, and CTA sections.
a11yNotes: Landmark regions: header, main, footer. Section headings follow a logical h1→h2→h3 hierarchy. CTA button has a descriptive aria-label.

positive: Use for product landing pages, feature announcement pages, and waitlist signups.
Customize headline and features array to match the product domain.
negative: Don't use inside an authenticated shell — MarketingPage includes its own Navbar and footer.

usageExamples:
import { MarketingPage } from '@zephrui/ui-react';
<MarketingPage headline='Ship faster' subheadline='Token-driven UI for vibe coders.' ctaLabel='Start building' onCta={() => router.push('/signup')} />
